fever.advice: Stay hydrated. Rest well. If fever exceeds 103°F or lasts more than 3 days, see a doctor.

headache.advice: Rest in a dark quiet room. Stay hydrated. Avoid screen time.

migraine.advice: Rest in dark quiet room. Avoid triggers like bright lights and loud sounds.

dizziness.advice: Sit or lie down immediately when dizzy. Avoid sudden movements. Stay hydrated. See doctor if recurrent.

dizzy.advice: Sit or lie down immediately. Avoid sudden head movements. Drink water. See doctor if this is recurrent.

vertigo.advice: Sit down immediately. Avoid sudden head movements. Epley maneuver can help BPPV. Consult ENT.

lightheaded.advice: Sit or lie down. Drink water or juice. Eat something if blood sugar is low.

spinning.advice: Lie still. Avoid sudden head movements. Consult a doctor if persistent.

cough.advice: Stay hydrated. Honey-ginger home remedy helps. See doctor if cough persists beyond 2 weeks.

dry cough.advice: Gargle with warm salt water. Steam inhalation helps.

wet cough.advice: Steam inhalation. Stay hydrated. See doctor if mucus is yellow/green or has blood.

cold.advice: Steam inhalation helps. Stay warm and rest. Vitamin C supplements may help.

runny nose.advice: Blow nose gently. Saline nasal drops help.

blocked nose.advice: Steam inhalation is very effective. Do not use decongestant sprays for more than 3 days.

sore throat.advice: Gargle with warm salt water every 4 hours. Avoid cold drinks.

throat pain.advice: Warm salt water gargles help. Avoid cold drinks.

stomach pain.advice: Eat light bland meals. If pain is severe or sudden, see a doctor urgently.

stomach ache.advice: Eat light bland food. Avoid spicy/oily food. See doctor if pain is severe.

tummy ache.advice: Rest. Light bland diet. See doctor if severe.

acidity.advice: Smaller meals. Avoid spicy/oily food. Don't lie down immediately after eating.

heartburn.advice: Avoid trigger foods. Eat smaller meals. Don't eat 2-3 hours before bedtime.

diarrhea.advice: Stay hydrated with ORS. Eat BRAT diet. See doctor if blood in stool.

loose motion.advice: Drink ORS. Eat light food. See doctor if persists beyond 2 days.

loose motions.advice: Stay hydrated with ORS. Eat BRAT diet. See doctor if blood in stool.

vomiting.advice: Sip small amounts of water frequently. See doctor if vomiting blood.

nausea.advice: Eat small bland meals. Avoid strong smells and greasy food.

motion sickness.advice: Take 30-60 minutes before travel. Sit in front seat. Focus on horizon.

back pain.advice: Apply warm compress. Gentle stretching. Avoid heavy lifting.

lower back pain.advice: Rest. Ice initially then warm compress. See doctor if pain radiates down leg.

neck pain.advice: Gentle neck exercises. Warm compress. Use a proper pillow.

shoulder pain.advice: Rest the shoulder. Apply ice for acute pain, heat for chronic.

knee pain.advice: Rest. Apply ice. Avoid stairs. Quadriceps exercises help.

skin rash.advice: Keep area clean and dry. Avoid scratching. See dermatologist if rash spreads.

itching.advice: Avoid scratching. Keep skin moisturized. Cool baths help.

fungal infection.advice: Keep area clean and dry. Apply antifungal cream twice daily. Wear loose cotton clothing.

eye irritation.advice: Do not rub eyes. Wash hands frequently.

red eye.advice: Do not rub. Seek medical attention if vision is affected.

toothache.advice: Rinse with warm salt water. See dentist as soon as possible.

ear pain.advice: Do not insert objects into ear. See a doctor — ear infections need antibiotics.

anxiety.advice: Practice deep breathing. Exercise regularly. Reduce caffeine. Consult a doctor for persistent anxiety.

stress.advice: Exercise regularly. Mindfulness. Maintain sleep schedule.

insomnia.advice: Consistent sleep schedule. Avoid screens before bed. Keep room dark and cool.

cant sleep.advice: Consistent sleep schedule. No screens 1 hour before bed. Dark, cool room.

joint pain.advice: Rest the joint. Alternate ice and heat.

chest pain.advice: URGENT: If severe, with arm/jaw radiation, sweating — call 112 immediately.

difficulty breathing.advice: URGENT: Seek immediate medical attention. Call 112 if severe.

shortness of breath.advice: URGENT: Sudden shortness of breath needs emergency evaluation. Call 112.

urinary infection.advice: Drink 2-3 litres of water. See doctor for antibiotic prescription.

burning urination.advice: Drink plenty of water. See a doctor for urine culture and antibiotics.

cold and fever.advice: Rest, stay hydrated. Consult doctor if symptoms worsen.

body ache.advice: Most body aches after exercise or a long day are completely normal — rest, light stretching, and staying hydrated usually fixes it within 24-48 hours. A warm bath or hot compress helps too. If the ache is accompanied by high fever, rash, or doesn't improve in 3 days, see a doctor.

fatigue.advice: 7-8 hours sleep. Stay hydrated. Balanced meals. Blood tests if fatigue persists.

tiredness.advice: Get adequate sleep. Eat iron-rich foods. Blood tests if persistent.

weakness.advice: Eat iron-rich foods. Stay hydrated. See doctor if sudden or severe.

constipation.advice: Drink 2-3 litres daily. High-fibre foods. Exercise regularly.

bloating.advice: Eat slowly. Avoid carbonated drinks, beans, cabbage. Walk after meals.

gas.advice: Avoid gas-producing foods. Eat slowly. Walk after meals.

palpitations.advice: Reduce caffeine. Manage stress. See doctor if frequent or with dizziness.

swelling.advice: Elevate swollen area. Reduce salt. See doctor for persistent or widespread swelling.

hair loss.advice: Protein-rich diet. Avoid heat styling. Get thyroid and iron tested.

dandruff.advice: Use medicated shampoo twice weekly. Leave on 5 minutes before rinsing.

period pain.advice: Warm compress on lower abdomen. Start pain relief a day before expected period.

menstrual cramps.advice: Heat pad. Take NSAID 1-2 days before period. Yoga reduces cramps long-term.

mouth ulcer.advice: Avoid spicy/acidic foods. Salt water rinse. See doctor if doesn't heal in 2 weeks.

acne.advice: Wash face twice daily. Do not pop pimples. Use oil-free sunscreen.

sunburn.advice: Cool water on skin. Aloe vera soothes. Avoid further sun exposure. Use SPF 50+.

dehydration.advice: Drink ORS. Avoid caffeine. See doctor if no urine output or confusion.

dengue.advice: URGENT: See doctor for platelet count. NEVER use Ibuprofen/Aspirin in dengue. Stay hydrated.

malaria.advice: URGENT: Requires blood smear and prescription antimalarials. See doctor immediately.

typhoid.advice: URGENT: Requires blood culture and prescription antibiotics. See doctor. Drink only boiled/purified water.

covid.advice: Isolate. Monitor SpO2 — seek emergency if below 94%. Consult doctor for treatment.

asthma.advice: Always carry rescue inhaler. Avoid triggers (dust, smoke, pollen). See pulmonologist.

allergies.advice: Identify and avoid allergens. Keep antihistamines handy.

depression.advice: Please seek professional help — psychiatrist or psychologist. Helpline: iCall 9152987821.

high blood pressure.advice: Reduce salt. Exercise. Avoid stress. See doctor — requires prescription medication.

thyroid.advice: Requires TSH, T3, T4 blood tests and prescription medication. See endocrinologist.

muscle pain.advice: Most body aches after exercise or a long day are completely normal — rest, light stretching, and staying hydrated usually fixes it within 24-48 hours. A warm bath or hot compress helps too. If the ache is accompanied by high fever, rash, or doesn't improve in 3 days, see a doctor.

muscle ache.advice: Most body aches after exercise or a long day are completely normal — rest, light stretching, and staying hydrated usually fixes it within 24-48 hours. A warm bath or hot compress helps too. If the ache is accompanied by high fever, rash, or doesn't improve in 3 days, see a doctor.

soreness.advice: Most body aches after exercise or a long day are completely normal — rest, light stretching, and staying hydrated usually fixes it within 24-48 hours. A warm bath or hot compress helps too. If the ache is accompanied by high fever, rash, or doesn't improve in 3 days, see a doctor.

head ache.advice: Rest in a dark quiet room. Stay hydrated. Avoid screen time.

headche.advice: Rest in a dark quiet room. Stay hydrated. Avoid screen time.

hedache.advice: Rest in a dark quiet room. Stay hydrated. Avoid screen time.

tummy pain.advice: Eat light bland meals. If pain is severe or sudden, see a doctor urgently.

back ache.advice: Apply warm compress. Gentle stretching. Avoid heavy lifting.

backache.advice: Apply warm compress. Gentle stretching. Avoid heavy lifting.

toothche.advice: Rinse with warm salt water. See dentist as soon as possible.

A warm bath or hot compress helps too. If the ache is accompanied by high fever, rash, or doesn't improve in 3 days, see a doctor.

cold and cough.advice: Steam inhalation helps. Stay warm and rest. Vitamin C supplements may help.

running nose.advice: Blow nose gently. Saline nasal drops help.

watery eyes.advice: Do not rub eyes. Wash hands frequently.

eye water.advice: Do not rub eyes. Wash hands frequently.

sore eyes.advice: Do not rub eyes. Wash hands frequently.

loose stool.advice: Stay hydrated with ORS. Eat BRAT diet. See doctor if blood in stool.

loose stools.advice: Stay hydrated with ORS. Eat BRAT diet. See doctor if blood in stool.

upset stomach.advice: Eat light bland meals. If pain is severe or sudden, see a doctor urgently.

upset tummy.advice: Eat light bland meals. If pain is severe or sudden, see a doctor urgently.

nauseus.advice: Eat small bland meals. Avoid strong smells and greasy food.

nauseous.advice: Eat small bland meals. Avoid strong smells and greasy food.

vomit.advice: Sip small amounts of water frequently. See doctor if vomiting blood.

throw up.advice: Sip small amounts of water frequently. See doctor if vomiting blood.

throwing up.advice: Sip small amounts of water frequently. See doctor if vomiting blood.

puking.advice: Sip small amounts of water frequently. See doctor if vomiting blood.

puke.advice: Sip small amounts of water frequently. See doctor if vomiting blood.

breathless.advice: URGENT: Sudden shortness of breath needs emergency evaluation. Call 112.

breathlessness.advice: URGENT: Sudden shortness of breath needs emergency evaluation. Call 112.

cant breathe.advice: URGENT: Seek immediate medical attention. Call 112 if severe.

difficulty to breathe.advice: URGENT: Seek immediate medical attention. Call 112 if severe.

pain in chest.advice: URGENT: If severe, with arm/jaw radiation, sweating — call 112 immediately.

chest tightness.advice: URGENT: If tightness is severe, with sweating or arm pain — call 112 immediately. For mild tightness after food, try antacid (Gelusil).

heart pain.advice: URGENT: If severe, with arm/jaw radiation, sweating — call 112 immediately.

leg pain.advice: Rest the joint. Alternate ice and heat.

knee ache.advice: Rest. Apply ice. Avoid stairs. Quadriceps exercises help.

shoulder ache.advice: Rest the shoulder. Apply ice for acute pain, heat for chronic.

neck ache.advice: Gentle neck exercises. Warm compress. Use a proper pillow.

skin problem.advice: Keep area clean and dry. Avoid scratching. See dermatologist if rash spreads.

itchy rash.advice: Keep area clean and dry. Avoid scratching. See dermatologist if rash spreads.

redness.advice: Keep area clean and dry. Avoid scratching. See dermatologist if rash spreads.

hair fall.advice: Protein-rich diet. Avoid heat styling. Get thyroid and iron tested.

falling hair.advice: Protein-rich diet. Avoid heat styling. Get thyroid and iron tested.

dandruf.advice: Use medicated shampoo twice weekly. Leave on 5 minutes before rinsing.

periods pain.advice: Warm compress on lower abdomen. Start pain relief a day before expected period.

period cramp.advice: Heat pad. Take NSAID 1-2 days before period. Yoga reduces cramps long-term.

mensuration pain.advice: Heat pad. Take NSAID 1-2 days before period. Yoga reduces cramps long-term.

menstruation pain.advice: Heat pad. Take NSAID 1-2 days before period. Yoga reduces cramps long-term.

menstrual pain.advice: Heat pad. Take NSAID 1-2 days before period. Yoga reduces cramps long-term.

high bp.advice: Reduce salt. Exercise. Avoid stress. See doctor — requires prescription medication.

low bp.advice: Sit or lie down immediately when dizzy. Avoid sudden movements. Stay hydrated. See doctor if recurrent.

thyroid problem.advice: Requires TSH, T3, T4 blood tests and prescription medication. See endocrinologist.

throat ache.advice: Gargle with warm salt water every 4 hours. Avoid cold drinks.

sore throut.advice: Gargle with warm salt water every 4 hours. Avoid cold drinks.

soar throat.advice: Gargle with warm salt water every 4 hours. Avoid cold drinks.

cold flu.advice: Rest, stay hydrated. Consult doctor if symptoms worsen.

flu.advice: Rest, stay hydrated. Consult doctor if symptoms worsen.

influenza.advice: Rest, stay hydrated. Consult doctor if symptoms worsen.

acne problem.advice: Wash face twice daily. Do not pop pimples. Use oil-free sunscreen.

pimple problem.advice: Wash face twice daily. Do not pop pimples. Use oil-free sunscreen.

zits.advice: Wash face twice daily. Do not pop pimples. Use oil-free sunscreen.

constipated.advice: Drink 2-3 litres daily. High-fibre foods. Exercise regularly.

no bowel.advice: Drink 2-3 litres daily. High-fibre foods. Exercise regularly.

cant poop.advice: Drink 2-3 litres daily. High-fibre foods. Exercise regularly.

gas problem.advice: Avoid gas-producing foods. Eat slowly. Walk after meals.

bloated feeling.advice: Eat slowly. Avoid carbonated drinks, beans, cabbage. Walk after meals.

feel bloated.advice: Eat slowly. Avoid carbonated drinks, beans, cabbage. Walk after meals.

anxiety attack.advice: Practice deep breathing. Exercise regularly. Reduce caffeine. Consult a doctor for persistent anxiety.

panic attack.advice: Practice deep breathing. Exercise regularly. Reduce caffeine. Consult a doctor for persistent anxiety.

feel anxious.advice: Practice deep breathing. Exercise regularly. Reduce caffeine. Consult a doctor for persistent anxiety.

feeling anxious.advice: Practice deep breathing. Exercise regularly. Reduce caffeine. Consult a doctor for persistent anxiety.

stress problem.advice: Exercise regularly. Mindfulness. Maintain sleep schedule.

sleepless.advice: Consistent sleep schedule. Avoid screens before bed. Keep room dark and cool.

no sleep.advice: Consistent sleep schedule. Avoid screens before bed. Keep room dark and cool.

sleep issues.advice: Consistent sleep schedule. Avoid screens before bed. Keep room dark and cool.

running fever.advice: Stay hydrated. Rest well. If fever exceeds 103°F or lasts more than 3 days, see a doctor.

mild fever.advice: Stay hydrated. Rest well. If fever exceeds 103°F or lasts more than 3 days, see a doctor.

high fever.advice: Stay hydrated. Rest well. If fever exceeds 103°F or lasts more than 3 days, see a doctor.

low fever.advice: Stay hydrated. Rest well. If fever exceeds 103°F or lasts more than 3 days, see a doctor.

slight fever.advice: Stay hydrated. Rest well. If fever exceeds 103°F or lasts more than 3 days, see a doctor.

body heat.advice: Stay hydrated. Rest well. If fever exceeds 103°F or lasts more than 3 days, see a doctor.

temperature.advice: Stay hydrated. Rest well. If fever exceeds 103°F or lasts more than 3 days, see a doctor.

muscle cramp.advice: Stretch the muscle gently and hold. Apply heat for relief. Drink water with a pinch of salt. Muscle cramps are usually harmless — caused by dehydration, low magnesium, or overuse. See doctor if cramps are frequent, severe, or in the chest.

muscle cramps.advice: Stretch the muscle gently and hold. Apply heat for relief. Drink water with a pinch of salt. Muscle cramps are usually harmless — caused by dehydration, low magnesium, or overuse. See doctor if cramps are frequent, severe, or in the chest.

cramp.advice: Stretch the muscle gently and hold. Apply heat for relief. Drink water with a pinch of salt. Muscle cramps are usually harmless — caused by dehydration, low magnesium, or overuse. See doctor if cramps are frequent, severe, or in the chest.

cramps.advice: Stretch the muscle gently and hold. Apply heat for relief. Drink water with a pinch of salt. Muscle cramps are usually harmless — caused by dehydration, low magnesium, or overuse. See doctor if cramps are frequent, severe, or in the chest.

leg cramp.advice: Stretch the muscle gently and hold. Apply heat for relief. Drink water with a pinch of salt. Muscle cramps are usually harmless — caused by dehydration, low magnesium, or overuse. See doctor if cramps are frequent, severe, or in the chest.

calf cramp.advice: Stretch the muscle gently and hold. Apply heat for relief. Drink water with a pinch of salt. Muscle cramps are usually harmless — caused by dehydration, low magnesium, or overuse. See doctor if cramps are frequent, severe, or in the chest.

ligament tear.advice: Apply RICE: Rest, Ice (20 min every hour), Compression (bandage), Elevation. Do NOT apply heat in first 48 hours. See an orthopaedic doctor urgently — ligament tears often need imaging (MRI) and may require physiotherapy or surgery.

torn ligament.advice: Apply RICE: Rest, Ice (20 min every hour), Compression (bandage), Elevation. Do NOT apply heat in first 48 hours. See an orthopaedic doctor urgently — ligament tears often need imaging (MRI) and may require physiotherapy or surgery.

ligament sprain.advice: Apply RICE: Rest, Ice (20 min every hour), Compression (bandage), Elevation. Do NOT apply heat in first 48 hours. See an orthopaedic doctor urgently — ligament tears often need imaging (MRI) and may require physiotherapy or surgery.

acl tear.advice: Apply RICE: Rest, Ice (20 min every hour), Compression (bandage), Elevation. Do NOT apply heat in first 48 hours. See an orthopaedic doctor urgently — ligament tears often need imaging (MRI) and may require physiotherapy or surgery.

sprain.advice: RICE method: Rest, Ice, Compression, Elevation. Avoid putting weight on it. See doctor if severe swelling or can't bear weight.

strain.advice: Rest the muscle. Apply ice for first 48 hours, then heat. Gentle stretching after 2-3 days. See doctor if severe.

eye pain.advice: Do NOT rub your eyes. Remove contact lenses if wearing any. Rinse with clean water if something is in the eye. See an eye doctor (ophthalmologist) if pain is severe, vision is affected, or there is discharge. Avoid screens for a while.

hand pain.advice: Rest the hand. Apply ice if swollen. Avoid repetitive movements. Wear a wrist brace if needed. See doctor if pain is severe, there is numbness, or fingers are tingling (could be carpal tunnel).

wrist pain.advice: Rest the wrist. Ice for swelling. Wrist brace helps. See doctor if pain is severe or persistent — could need X-ray.

finger pain.advice: Rest the finger. Ice if swollen. See doctor if finger is bent abnormally, very swollen, or you cannot straighten it.

foot pain.advice: Rest feet. Use cushioned footwear. Stretch calf muscles. Ice for swelling. See doctor if pain is severe or you cannot walk.

heel pain.advice: Stretch before walking. Use heel cushion insoles. Avoid walking barefoot on hard floors. See doctor if persistent.

hip pain.advice: Rest. Avoid activities that worsen pain. See doctor — hip pain especially in elderly needs urgent evaluation (could be fracture).
